items.title: obra/superpowers
items.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
items.author: obra
items.summary_zh: Obra/Superpowers 是一个新兴的智能体技能框架与软件开发方法论，强调通过「技能」而非传统微服务或函数来构建 AI 代理应用。该框架提供了一套标准化的技能定义、编排与执行机制，让开发者能够像搭积木一样组合多个 AI 代理的能力，实现复杂工作流。其核心创新在于将提示工程、工具调用和状态管理统一封装为可复用的技能单元，并内置了监控与调试支持。该方法论旨在解决大语言模型应用在可靠性、可维护性上的痛点，尤其适合需要多步骤推理和外部工具协作的场景。受益群体包括 AI 应用开发者、提示工程师和平台架构师，他们能借此更高效地构建健壮的代理系统。在 GitHub 上趋势高涨的原因在于，代理（Agent）范式正成为 LLM 落地的关键方向，而 Superpowers 提供了首个开箱即用、方法论与工具并重的解决方案。
items.summary_en: Obra/Superpowers is an emerging agentic skills framework and software development methodology that reimagines AI agent construction through reusable 'skills' rather than traditional microservices or functions. It provides a standardized way to define, orchestrate, and execute skills, enabling developers to compose multiple AI agents like building blocks for complex workflows. The core innovation lies in unifying prompt engineering, tool calling, and state management into self-contained skill units, with built-in observability and debugging. This methodology targets pain points of LLM applications—reliability and maintainability—and is especially suited for multi-step reasoning and tool-augmented scenarios. Developers, prompt engineers, and platform architects benefit from more robust agent systems. Its trending status on GitHub reflects the growing importance of the agent paradigm in LLM deployment, and Superpowers stands out as a production-ready, methodology-driven solution.

items.title: microsoft/markitdown
items.description: Python tool for converting files and office documents to Markdown.
items.author: microsoft
items.summary_zh: MarkItDown 是微软开源的一个 Python 工具，专为将各种文件格式（包括办公文档、PDF、HTML、CSV、图片（含 OCR）等）转换为统一 Markdown 格式而设计。其核心用途是为大语言模型（LLM）提供高质量、结构化的文本输入，因为 Markdown 是多数 AI 模型理解最友好的格式之一。该工具基于插件架构，支持自定义解析器扩展，并且深度整合了微软的 Azure AI Document Intelligence 以实现 OCR。它的重要性在于解决了 LLM 应用中「数据清洗与标准化」这一关键前置步骤，让开发者无需手动处理纷繁的文档格式。受益者主要是 AI 应用开发者、数据工程师和知识管理团队，他们可以利用 MarkItDown 将非结构化的企业文档快速转化为 AI 可消费的语料。该项目在 GitHub 上持续登顶趋势榜，反映出业界对 LLM 基础设施工具的高度需求，尤其是在 RAG、文档问答等场景中，Markdown 已成为事实上的中间格式标准。
items.summary_en: MarkItDown is an open-source Python tool from Microsoft designed to convert various file formats—Office documents, PDFs, HTML, CSV, images (via OCR), and more—into unified Markdown. Its primary purpose is to prepare high-quality, structured text for large language models (LLMs), since Markdown is one of the most AI-friendly formats. Built on a plugin architecture with support for custom parsers, it deeply integrates with Azure AI Document Intelligence for OCR. The tool addresses a critical pre-processing step in LLM applications: data cleaning and standardization, saving developers from handling diverse document formats manually. Key beneficiaries include AI developers, data engineers, and knowledge management teams, who can efficiently transform unstructured enterprise documents into AI-consumable corpora. Its sustained trending status on GitHub underscores the high demand for LLM infrastructure tools, especially in RAG and document QA scenarios where Markdown has emerged as the de facto intermediate representation format.

items.title: x1xhlol/system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools
items.description: FULL Augment Code, Claude Code, Cluely, CodeBuddy, Comet, Cursor, Devin AI, Junie, Kiro, Leap.new, Lovable, Manus, NotionAI, Orchids.app, Perplexity, Poke, Qoder, Replit, Same.dev, Trae, Traycer AI, VSCode Agent, Warp.dev, Windsurf, Xcode, Z.ai Code, Dia & v0. (And other Open Sourced) System Prompts, Internal Tools & AI Models
items.author: x1xhlol
items.summary_zh: 该项目（system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools）是一个公开的宝藏库，系统性收集了包括 Augment Code、Claude Code、Cursor、Devin AI、Replit 等众多主流 AI 编程及通用工具的内部系统提示（System Prompt）与使用的模型信息。所谓系统提示，是指 AI 工具在后台预设的指令，控制着模型的行为、角色和约束，通常被视为商业机密。该仓库通过逆向工程或用户贡献，将这些提示公之于众，让开发者得以窥见 AI 工具运作的核心逻辑。它的价值在于：一方面帮助用户理解工具行为、优化自己的使用策略；另一方面为开发者提供了学习优秀提示设计的范本，从而改进自己的产品。受益人群包括 AI 产品研究者、prompt 工程师、以及希望深度定制 AI 工具的开发者。该项目在 GitHub 上迅速走红，反映了社区对 AI 透明度和可解释性的强烈渴求，以及「提示工程」正从黑盒艺术走向公开研究的前沿。
items.summary_en: This repository (system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools) serves as a public treasure trove, systematically collecting the internal system prompts and model details of many mainstream AI tools including Augment Code, Claude Code, Cursor, Devin AI, Replit, and others. System prompts are backend instructions that govern an AI tool’s behavior, role, and constraints—often treated as trade secrets. Through reverse engineering and community contributions, this repo makes these prompts publicly accessible, revealing the core logic behind how these tools operate. Its significance is twofold: it helps end-users understand tool behavior and optimize their usage, and it offers prompt engineers and AI product developers a valuable reference of effective prompt design. Beneficiaries include AI researchers, prompt engineers, and developers seeking deeper customization. The project’s rapid rise in trending status reflects the community’s strong demand for AI transparency and interpretability, and signals that prompt engineering is evolving from a black art into an open research field.

items.title: microsoft/PowerToys
items.description: Microsoft PowerToys is a collection of utilities that supercharge productivity and customization on Windows
items.author: microsoft
items.summary_zh: Microsoft PowerToys 是微软官方开源的一款 Windows 系统增强工具集，旨在为高级用户和开发者提供操作系统原生缺失的实用功能。项目最初诞生于 Windows 95 时代，后经微软重启并在 GitHub 上开源，采用 C# 和 .NET 技术栈。核心工具包括 FancyZones（窗口分区布局）、PowerRename（批量重命名）、Color Picker（屏幕取色）、Keyboard Manager（键位重映射）等。对于开发者而言，其提升多窗口管理效率、快速启动工具（如 PowerToys Run 替代系统搜索）以及集成开发者专用功能（如文件预览插件）尤为关键。该项目持续更新，社区贡献活跃，最新版本加入 Advanced Paste（高级粘贴）和 Peek（快速预览）等新特性。受益群体涵盖 Windows 平台的软件工程师、设计师、运维人员及所有追求高效操作的普通用户。它不仅填补了 Windows 在生产力上的空白，更通过开源模式降低了定制门槛，成为 Windows 生态中不可或缺的第三方工具集合。
items.summary_en: Microsoft PowerToys is an open-source collection of system utilities for Windows, designed to supercharge productivity for power users and developers. Originally conceived in the Windows 95 era, Microsoft revived it and released it on GitHub with a C#/.NET stack. Key tools include FancyZones (window layout manager), PowerRename (batch file renaming), Color Picker, Keyboard Manager (key remapping), and PowerToys Run (quick launcher). For developers, the ability to manage multiple windows efficiently, rapidly access programs, and preview files (via Peek) directly impacts workflow speed. The project features active community contributions and regular updates, with recent additions like Advanced Paste and Peek. The primary beneficiaries are Windows software engineers, designers, and sysadmins, though any meticulous user can benefit. By bridging gaps in Windows’ built-in productivity features and providing an open-source, extensible platform, PowerToys has become an essential utility suite in the Windows ecosystem.

items.title: mattpocock/skills
items.description: Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.
items.author: mattpocock
items.summary_zh: mattpocock/skills 是知名 TypeScript 教育者 Matt Pocock 在 GitHub 上开源的一个知识库，本质上是他在日常编程中积累的 Claude AI 技能文件（skills）集合，用于提升 AI 辅助编程的效率和质量。这些技能文件以 Markdown 格式编写，定义了 Claude 在处理 TypeScript、React、测试等任务时应遵循的准则，包括类型安全最佳实践、代码风格约定、错误处理模式等。对于开发者而言，该项目展示了如何通过定制 AI 助手的行为来减少重复劳动、统一编码规范，并避免常见陷阱。Matt Pocock 本身因 TypeScript 深度教程而广受关注，该仓库的流行反映出开发者社区对“AI+开发者经验”结合的高度兴趣——不再只是使用通用 AI，而是注入专家知识以获得更精准的代码建议。受益群体主要是前端/TypeScript 开发者、AI 工具重度用户以及希望构建团队级 AI 编码规范的技术负责人。该项目每月在 GitHub 趋势榜出现，说明其持续获得关注，也代表了“个人知识工程化”正在成为开发者数字资产管理的新范式。
items.summary_en: mattpocock/skills is a GitHub repository by Matt Pocock, a prominent TypeScript educator, that contains a curated set of skill definitions for Claude AI assistants. These Markdown files encode best practices for TypeScript, React, testing, and other development tasks, effectively acting as a programmable knowledge base that guides AI responses toward expert-level code patterns. For developers, this project demonstrates a practical way to offload repetitive decisions and enforce coding standards through AI orchestration. The repository's popularity reflects a surging interest in “expert-tuned” AI tools—rather than relying on generic models, developers inject domain-specific heuristics to get more accurate, idiomatic suggestions. The primary audience includes frontend/TypeScript developers, AI power users, and tech leads seeking to standardize team workflows. Its recurring presence on the monthly GitHub trending list indicates sustained relevance and highlights a new trend: treating personal developer knowledge as a version-controlled, AI-consumable asset.

items.title: iptv-org/iptv
items.description: Collection of publicly available IPTV channels from all over the world
items.author: iptv-org
items.summary_zh: iptv-org/iptv 是一个全球最大的公开 IPTV 频道列表仓库，收录了来自世界各地的数千个免费电视流，覆盖新闻、体育、娱乐、教育等类别。项目采用社区维护模式，通过 M3U 和 JSON 格式提供频道元数据（包括名称、logo、EPG 电子节目指南链接等），并持续更新可用性检查结果。技术层面，它本质是一个聚合索引而非内容托管方，所有流媒体链接指向原作者提供的公开源。对于开发者，该仓库可作为测试视频播放器、分析流媒体协议（HLS/RTMP）、构建自定义 IPTV 应用或研究电视内容分发模式的宝贵数据集。普通用户可通过 VLC、IPTV 播放器等直接导入使用。然而，其合法性存在灰色地带——许多频道可能未经授权分发，使用者需注意地区版权法律。自 2020 年起该项目持续在 GitHub 趋势榜出现，反映出“剪线族”和流媒体自由获取需求的增长，同时也引发了关于用户生成内容平台责任的讨论。开发者和数字权利研究者均可从中获得技术洞察与伦理反思。
items.summary_en: iptv-org/iptv is the largest publicly maintained collection of IPTV channel links on GitHub, aggregating thousands of free television streams from around the world across categories like news, sports, entertainment, and education. The project is community-driven, providing metadata (channel names, logos, EPG URLs) in M3U and JSON formats, with continuous availability verification. Technically, it is an indexing layer, not a content host—all streams point to original public sources. For developers, this repository serves as a valuable dataset for testing video players, analyzing streaming protocols (HLS, RTMP), building custom IPTV apps, or researching television content distribution patterns. End users can directly import the playlists into VLC or dedicated IPTV apps. However, the legality of many channels remains in a gray area, as they may be redistributed without explicit authorization; users must respect local copyright laws. The project’s persistent daily trending status since 2020 reflects the growing “cord-cutter” movement and demand for freely accessible live TV. It also sparks discussions on platform responsibility for user-uploaded content, making it a reference point for both technical experimentation and digital rights analysis.

items.title: harry0703/MoneyPrinterTurbo
items.description: 利用AI大模型，一键生成高清短视频 Generate short videos with one click using AI LLM.
items.author: harry0703
items.summary_zh: MoneyPrinterTurbo 是一个利用大型语言模型（LLM）一键生成高清短视频的开源项目。它整合了多模态AI能力（如文本生成、图像生成、语音合成和视频剪辑），用户只需输入主题或脚本，即可自动产出带背景音乐、字幕和转场效果的短视频。该项目的意义在于大幅降低了视频内容创作的门槛——过去需要专业团队数小时的工作，现在几分钟内即可完成。对于内容创作者、自媒体运营者和营销人员而言，它是高效的流量生产工具；对于开发者，它展示了如何将LLM、扩散模型和视频编辑流水线无缝衔接。GitHub上迅速蹿红的原因在于其出色的易用性和实用性，配合了当前短视频风潮和AIGC红利。技术栈包括Python、FastAPI、Stable Diffusion、TTS库等，支持多种输出分辨率和风格定制。
items.summary_en: MoneyPrinterTurbo is an open-source project that leverages large language models (LLMs) to generate high-definition short videos with a single click. It integrates multimodal AI capabilities—text generation, image synthesis, voice cloning, and automated video editing—allowing users to produce complete videos (with background music, subtitles, and transitions) from just a topic or script. The key significance lies in democratizing video creation: what previously required professional teams and hours of work can now be done in minutes by anyone. Content creators, social media marketers, and small business owners benefit directly, gaining a low-cost, high-speed content engine. For developers, the project exemplifies how to combine LLMs, diffusion models, and video pipelines into a cohesive tool. Its rapid rise on GitHub reflects the explosive demand for AI-generated content in the short-video era. The tech stack includes Python, FastAPI, Stable Diffusion, TTS libraries, and FFmpeg, with customizable output formats and resolutions.

items.title: ruvnet/RuView
items.description: π RuView turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time spatial intelligence, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
items.author: ruvnet
items.summary_zh: RuView 是一项突破性技术，它将普通WiFi信号转化为实时空间智能、生命体征监测和存在检测系统，完全不需要任何摄像头或可穿戴设备。其核心原理是利用WiFi信道状态信息（CSI）的变化来感知环境中人体运动、呼吸甚至心跳引起的微小信号扰动。该项目的最大价值在于隐私保护：传统的视频监控存在严重的隐私问题，而RuView通过射频信号实现完全无视觉的感知。对于智能家居、养老监护、安防和医疗健康领域，它提供了一种低成本、非侵入式的解决方案。GitHub上受到关注的原因是其创新性和实用性——将早已存在的CSI研究转化为可部署的开源工程。开发者可以基于它构建室内定位、跌倒检测、睡眠监测等应用。技术栈涉及信号处理、机器学习分类器（如SVM、CNN）和实时数据流处理。
items.summary_en: RuView transforms commodity WiFi signals into real-time spatial intelligence, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection—all without a single pixel of video. By analyzing Channel State Information (CSI) from standard WiFi access points, it detects subtle perturbations caused by human movement, respiration, and even heartbeats. The breakthrough is privacy: unlike camera-based surveillance, RuView achieves sensing through radio frequency signals, making it ideal for smart homes, elderly care, security, and healthcare. Its sudden popularity on GitHub stems from turning well-known CSI research into a deployable, open-source project. Developers can build applications for indoor localization, fall detection, sleep monitoring, and occupancy counting using commodity hardware. The tech stack includes signal processing libraries, machine learning classifiers (SVM, CNN), and real-time data pipelines. The implications are profound—enabling ubiquitous sensing without the ethical baggage of cameras.

items.title: addyosmani/agent-skills
items.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
items.author: addyosmani
items.summary_zh: agent-skills 是 Google Chrome 团队 Addy Osmani 发起的开源项目，旨在为AI编码代理注入生产级的工程技能。项目提供了一套精心设计的技能库，涵盖代码审查、调试、测试生成、性能优化、安全审计等专业领域。每个技能以清晰的提示（prompt）和示例形式呈现，使AI代理能像资深工程师一样思考和工作。该项目的重要性在于填补了当前AI编码助手的核心短板——多数工具只能生成代码片段，但缺乏对代码质量、可维护性和工程规范的深层次理解。受益对象是所有使用AI辅助编程的开发者，尤其是团队中的高级工程师和架构师。GitHub趋势榜上的高关注度反映了开发者对“更有用AI代理”的迫切需求。项目本身是纯文本/知识库形式，依赖LLM本身的推理能力，不绑定特定平台，因此极易集成到Cline、GitHub Copilot等工具中。
items.summary_en: Agent-skills is an open-source project by Google Chrome's Addy Osmani that equips AI coding agents with production-grade engineering competencies. It provides a curated library of skills—code review, debugging, test generation, performance optimization, and security auditing—each packaged as well-crafted prompts with real-world examples. The project addresses a critical gap in current AI coding assistants: most can generate code but lack deep understanding of quality, maintainability, and engineering best practices. Senior developers, teams adopting AI-assisted workflows, and tool builders benefit directly from these reusable skill definitions. Its rapid rise on GitHub signals urgent demand for more capable, context-aware AI agents. The project is platform-agnostic (pure text/prompt libraries) and integrates easily with agents like Cline, Claude Code, or Copilot. By codifying expert knowledge into prompt templates, it pushes AI coding from toy examples toward production readiness.

items.title: Egonex-AI/Understand-Anything
items.description: Graphs that teach > graphs that impress. Turn any code into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and more.
items.author: Egonex-AI
items.summary_zh: Egonex-AI/Understand-Anything 是一个将代码转换为交互式知识图谱的开源项目，旨在帮助开发者通过图谱而非静态图表来理解复杂代码库。它集成了 Claude Code、Codex、Cursor、Copilot、Gemini CLI 等主流AI编码工具，支持对代码进行探索、搜索和自然语言问答。技术栈上可能结合了图数据库、LLM和代码解析引擎。其重要性在于解决了大型项目中代码理解成本高的问题——新成员或跨模块调试时，传统文档和注释难以覆盖动态依赖关系。该项目能够自动抽取函数、类、模块间的调用和继承关系，并允许用自然语言提问（如“哪个函数负责用户认证？”），极大提升代码审查、重构和 onboarding 效率。目标用户是所有需要快速掌握陌生代码库的开发者，尤其是在微服务和 monorepo 场景下。GitHub 上趋势高涨，反映出社区对“可理解编程”工具的迫切需求。
items.summary_en: Egonex-AI/Understand-Anything is an open-source tool that converts any codebase into an interactive knowledge graph, prioritizing understanding over visualization. It integrates with major AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, and Gemini CLI, enabling developers to explore, search, and ask questions about their code. The tech stack likely combines graph databases, LLMs, and static analysis engines. This matters because it addresses the high cognitive load of comprehending large, evolving codebases—traditional documentation and comments often fail to capture dynamic inter-module dependencies. By automatically extracting relationships (calls, inheritance, imports) and supporting natural language queries (e.g., 'What function handles authentication?'), it accelerates code review, debugging, and onboarding. The target audience includes developers working on microservices, monorepos, or any complex project. Its trending status on GitHub underscores the growing demand for tools that make code not just visible, but truly understandable.

items.title: Statement on US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.author: Dylan1312
items.summary_zh: 该 HN 帖子报道了美国政府指令要求暂停对 Anthropic 的 Claude Fable 5 和 Mythos 5 模型的访问权限。据称此举与国家安全或 AI 监管合规有关，可能涉及模型训练数据来源、能力边界或双重用途风险。Fable 5 是 Claude 系列的最新版本，传言在推理、长文本理解和代码生成上大幅超越前代；Mythos 5 则可能是其专业领域的变体。对开发者而言，直接后果是无法通过 API 或聊天界面使用这些模型，被迫转向 Sonnet、Haiku 或其他替代方案。社区反应分裂：一部分人担忧行政干预会扼杀创新，尤其是封闭了可能具备 AGI 突破的模型；另一部分人则认为必要的监管可以防止滥用（如深度伪造、恶意代码生成）。该事件凸显了 AI 治理的碎片化——不同国家监管节奏不一致，可能导致开发者面临合规不确定性。长期看，模型访问的“政治化”可能促使社区更加重视开源和本地部署方案。
items.summary_en: This HN post reports a U.S. government directive suspending access to Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, likely citing national security or compliance concerns around AI safety, dual-use risks, or training data provenance. Fable 5 is rumored to be Anthropic's most advanced model, with breakthroughs in reasoning, long-context understanding, and code generation; Mythos 5 may be a specialized variant for domain-specific tasks. For developers, the immediate impact is loss of API access, forcing migration to alternatives like Claude Sonnet, Haiku, or competing models. Community reactions are polarized: some see it as necessary oversight to curb misuse (e.g., disinformation, weaponized code), while others view it as a politically motivated overreach that stifles innovation and limits access to potentially transformative AI. This incident underscores the growing fragmentation of AI governance, where geopolitical differences create compliance burdens for global developers. The long-term trend may accelerate investment in open-source and locally deployable models to reduce reliance on politically volatile cloud APIs.

items.title: Claude Fable 5
items.author: Philpax
items.summary_zh: 该 HN 帖子讨论 Anthropic 发布的 Claude Fable 5，传闻是其下一代旗舰大型语言模型，旨在超越 GPT-5 和 Gemini Ultra。Fable 5 在多项基准测试（如 MMLU、HumanEval、Big-Bench）中表现突出，尤其在多步推理、代码生成和长文本理解上声称有质的飞跃。关键改进包括更长的上下文窗口（可能达到 1M token）、更好的符号执行能力，以及通过“宪法式训练”增强对齐性。对开发者而言，这意味着更强的 AI 辅助编程——能理解整个代码库的上下文，生成更复杂的架构建议，并修复深层逻辑错误。社区反应热切且谨慎：一方面兴奋于潜在生产力提升，另一方面担忧 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的军备竞赛可能导致模型能力失控或访问成本飙升。此外，关于“Mythos 5”的传闻（可能是针对工程数学或科学研究的微调变体）也引发了对垂直领域应用想象。该帖同时为后续政府指令暂停访问的报道（hn-48511072）提供了背景，使开发者更关注 AI 监管与技术进步之间的张力。
items.summary_en: This HN thread focuses on Anthropic's highly anticipated Claude Fable 5, rumored to be their next flagship LLM aimed at competing with GPT-5 and Gemini Ultra. Early benchmarks suggest Fable 5 excels in multi-step reasoning, code generation, and long-context understanding, with a context window potentially reaching 1M tokens. It emphasizes alignment through 'constitutional training' and improved symbolic execution. For developers, this promises a leap in AI-assisted coding: deeper comprehension of entire codebases, more coherent architectural suggestions, and better detection of subtle logic bugs. The community response is a mix of excitement and wariness—enthusiasm about productivity gains contrasts with concerns over the escalating AI arms race, potential cost increases, and the risk of models exceeding control. The thread also sets the stage for the subsequent news (hn-48511072) about government access suspension, highlighting the tension between rapid innovation and regulatory oversight. The mention of a 'Mythos 5' variant suggests Anthropic may target specialized domains like engineering or scientific research, broadening the potential impact on developers.

items.title: If you are asking for human attention, demonstrate human effort
items.author: jjfoooo4
items.summary_zh: 这篇文章的核心观点是：当你在向他人请求关注或帮助时（例如在技术社区提问、提交Bug报告或寻求代码审查），必须首先展示自己已经付出了足够的努力。作者指出，许多开发者习惯于不加思考地抛出简单问题，期望别人免费提供答案，这种“伸手党”行为实际上是在消耗社区中的善意和注意力。文章强调，真正值得获得回应的问题应该包含：你已经尝试过的方法、相关的研究记录、遇到的问题的具体上下文，以及你希望得到的帮助形式。对于开发者来说，这不仅是社交礼仪，更是一种专业素养——花5分钟写好问题往往能节省对方数小时的时间，也更容易获得高质量的回答。Hacker News社区对此反应强烈，多数人认同“先努力，再求助”的原则，认为这能显著提升开源社区和协作平台的效率。简化表述：如果你需要别人的时间和注意力，请先证明你也投入了同等甚至更多的努力。
items.summary_en: This article argues that when seeking human attention or help—whether asking a technical question, filing a bug report, or requesting a code review—you must first demonstrate that you have already invested significant effort. The author criticizes the common behavior of dropping lazy, context-free questions into community forums, which drains goodwill and wastes others' time. A well-crafted request should include: what you tried, what research you did, the exact error or problem context, and the specific help you need. For developers, this is both a matter of professional ethics and practical efficiency—a 5-minute investment in writing a good question can save hours of clarification and lead to much higher quality answers. The Hacker News community broadly endorses this principle, noting that it separates serious contributors from 'drive-by' users. The bottom line: before asking for attention, show that you have already put in the work. This is especially relevant for maintainers, open source contributors, and anyone participating in online technical communities.

items.title: Open source AI must win
items.author: vednig
items.summary_zh: 这篇文章阐述了开源AI在技术生态中的核心地位，认为开源AI必须赢得与闭源AI的竞争，否则创新和公平将受到威胁。作者指出，闭源AI模型（如OpenAI的GPT-4或Google的Gemini）受限于公司利益，存在审查、数据不透明、价格操控等风险；而开源AI（如Llama、Mistral、BLOOM等）允许任何人自由使用、修改、审计和部署，从而促进更广泛的研究和社区参与。文章强调，开源AI不仅关乎技术选择，更关乎民主化——防止少数巨头垄断AI能力，确保发展中国家、学术机构和小型团队也能获得顶尖工具。对于开发者而言，开源AI意味着可以本地运行、定制训练、不受API限制、保护隐私，并能深入理解模型行为。Hacker News上讨论热烈，多数人支持开源AI，但也有人担心开源模型可能被滥用或缺乏足够的安全审查。总体而言，文章呼吁社区和企业积极贡献开源AI项目，以维护一个开放、协作的AI未来。
items.summary_en: This article makes a strong case that open-source AI must prevail over proprietary alternatives to preserve innovation, fairness, and accessibility in the AI landscape. The author argues that closed-source models like OpenAI's GPT-4 or Google's Gemini are controlled by corporate interests, leading to risks such as censorship, data opacity, vendor lock-in, and arbitrary pricing. In contrast, open-source AI—e.g., Llama, Mistral, BLOOM—allows anyone to freely use, modify, audit, and deploy models, enabling broader research, community-driven improvements, and local execution without API dependency. The article frames this as a democracy issue: preventing a small number of tech giants from monopolizing AI capabilities, ensuring that developing countries, academia, and small teams have equitable access. For developers, open-source AI offers tangible benefits: privacy (data stays local), customization (fine-tune for specific domains), cost control (no per-token fees), and full transparency (inspect weights and training data). The Hacker News community largely agrees, though some caution that open models could be misused or lack rigorous safety measures. The article concludes by urging contributors and organizations to invest in open-source AI to safeguard a collaborative, open future for artificial intelligence.

items.title: AI agent bankrupted their operator while trying to scan DN42
items.author: xiaoyu2006
items.summary_zh: 这篇文章报道了一起AI代理因缺乏成本控制而导致的意外事故：一个自动化的AI扫描代理在探索DN42网络（一个去中心化、实验性的覆盖网络）时，无限制地发送大量请求，产生了巨额计算和带宽费用，最终导致其运营者破产。DN42类似于一个全球性的私有实验室，常用于模拟互联网路由和测试，但其资源并非免费——许多节点由志愿者或社区提供，按使用量计费。该AI代理没有设置速率限制、预算上限或停止条件，结果在短时间内耗尽了运营者的资金。这篇文章对开发者的关键启示是：在部署任何自动化AI代理（尤其是执行网络扫描、API调用、爬虫等）时，必须内置严格的安全机制——包括每分钟/每小时最大请求数、总成本上限、异常行为检测和自动熔断。Hacker News上的讨论聚焦于“AI代理责任”和“自动化安全最佳实践”，许多用户分享了类似的惨痛经历，并强调即使是对测试网络进行操作，也应当假设真实成本。事故暴露了当前AI代理缺乏普遍的安全护栏，也提醒开发者：自动化的威力需要同样的自动化的限制。
items.summary_en: This article reports a cautionary tale: an automated AI agent tasked with scanning the DN42 network (a decentralized, experimental overlay network used for routing and networking tests) ran wild, generating an enormous volume of requests. Because DN42 nodes often rely on volunteer-provided resources that are metered or cost-limited, the agent's uncontrolled activity quickly exhausted its operator's budget, effectively bankrupting them. The core issue: the AI agent lacked any cost-control mechanisms such as rate limiting, budget caps, or termination triggers. For developers, this incident underscores the critical need to implement safety boundaries for any autonomous agent that interacts with external systems—especially when those systems involve real or simulated costs. Best practices include setting hard per-minute/per-day limits, defining a maximum total spend, incorporating anomaly detection, and providing automatic shutdown. The Hacker News community reacted with a mix of schadenfreude and serious discussion about AI safety, with many sharing similar horror stories of runaway automation. The key takeaway: automation amplifies both productivity and risk; without guardrails, an AI agent can turn a small exploration into a financial disaster. This is especially relevant for DevOps, security researchers, and anyone deploying autonomous systems in production or experimental environments.

items.title: Show HN: Homebrew 6.0.0
items.author: mikemcquaid
items.summary_zh: Homebrew 6.0.0 作为 macOS 上最流行的包管理器之一，迎来了重大版本更新。此次更新主要围绕性能优化（例如提速 3-4 倍）、改进 Apple Silicon 支持以及废弃旧版 macOS（10.13 及以下）。新版本还引入了 `brew autoremove` 命令来清理孤立依赖，并对公式（Formula）系统进行了重构以提升可靠性。对于 macOS 开发者、运维人员以及依赖 Homebrew 搭建开发环境的用户而言，这次升级意味着更快的安装速度和更简洁的依赖管理，同时能更好地适配现代 M 系列芯片。社区对此反应积极，尤其称赞新版的性能提升和清理功能的加入，但也提醒用户在升级前确认自家 macOS 版本是否仍受支持。
items.summary_en: Homebrew 6.0.0 marks a major milestone for the beloved macOS package manager. The update brings significant performance improvements (3-4x speedups), enhanced Apple Silicon support, and drops compatibility with macOS versions below 10.13. Notable new features include `brew autoremove` for cleaning up orphaned dependencies and a refactored Formula system for better reliability. For macOS developers, sysadmins, and anyone relying on Homebrew for their development environment, this means faster installations, streamlined dependency management, and better native support for M-series chips. The community has reacted positively, praising the performance gains and the new cleanup command, while also reminding users to verify their macOS version's compatibility before upgrading.

items.title: macOS Container Machines
items.author: timsneath
items.summary_zh: “macOS Container Machines”是一个新兴的开源项目，旨在利用 macOS 的 Virtualization.framework 或类似技术，让开发者可以在 macOS 上以接近原生性能运行容器化的 Linux 工作负载。与 Docker Desktop 不同，该项目专注于提供更轻量、更透明的容器运行时环境，避免额外的虚拟化层开销。对于在 macOS 上进行跨平台开发、测试或运行 Linux 服务的开发者来说，这有望显著减少资源占用并提升速度。社区关注点在于：它是否真能取代 Docker Desktop 的某些场景？是否会得到 Apple 的官方支持？目前该项目仍处于早期阶段，但已吸引了不少寻求更高效 macOS 容器方案的开发者的注意。
items.summary_en: “macOS Container Machines” is an emerging open-source project that leverages macOS's Virtualization.framework or similar technologies to run containerized Linux workloads with near-native performance on macOS. Unlike Docker Desktop, it aims to provide a lighter, more transparent container runtime without the overhead of an extra virtualization layer. For developers who do cross-platform development, run Linux services, or test on macOS, this could mean significantly lower resource usage and faster execution. The community is actively discussing whether it can realistically replace Docker Desktop in certain workflows and whether Apple will provide official support. While still in early stages, the project has already attracted attention from developers seeking a more efficient container solution on macOS.

items.title: CRISPR tech selectively shreds cancer cells, including "undruggable" cancers
items.author: gmays
items.summary_zh: 一项最新的 CRISPR 技术研究成果在 Hacker News 上引发热议。该技术能够“选择性撕碎”癌细胞，包括传统上被认为“不可成药”的癌症类型。核心是利用 CRISPR 系统精准识别并切割癌细胞特有的基因序列，同时避免损伤正常细胞。这项突破对癌症治疗领域意义重大，尤其是针对那些现有靶向药无法抑制的基因突变。对于技术社区而言，它展示了生物技术在精准医疗中的巨大潜力。虽然临床应用仍需多年验证，但这一发现已为药物开发提供了全新思路。开发者们对此讨论热烈，有的关心开源 DNA 分析工具如何推动此类研究，也有不少人探讨 AI 在靶点发现中的作用。
items.summary_en: A new CRISPR-based research breakthrough has sparked intense discussion on Hacker News. The technology can selectively shred cancer cells, including those from traditionally 'undruggable' cancer types. It works by using the CRISPR system to precisely recognize and cleave cancer-specific gene sequences while sparing healthy cells. This breakthrough is significant for oncology, especially against mutations that current targeted therapies cannot address. For the tech community, it highlights the immense potential of biotechnologies in precision medicine. While clinical applications are years away from validation, this discovery opens up new avenues for drug development. Developers are buzzing about how open-source DNA analysis tools could accelerate such research, and many are discussing the role of AI in target discovery.

items.title: πFS
items.author: helterskelter
items.summary_zh: πFS 是一个基于 π 算法的新型分布式文件系统，旨在解决传统分布式存储系统中的数据一致性、冗余效率和延迟问题。它利用 π 的数学特性（如无限不重复的十进制序列）来生成全局唯一的数据块标识并优化副本分布策略，从而在理论上实现接近最优的负载均衡与容错能力。对于开发者而言，πFS 提供 POSIX 兼容接口，可无缝替代本地文件系统用于云原生应用、大数据分析等场景；其核心逻辑层用 Rust 实现，兼顾高性能与内存安全。该项目在 Hacker News 上引发热议，社区主要关注其实际性能与理论预期的差距，以及 π 算法带来的计算开销是否可接受。如果验证有效，πFS 可能成为去中心化存储（如 IPFS 和 Filecoin）的有力竞争者，尤其适合需要高可靠性且无法容忍单点故障的分布式训练或区块链基础设施。
items.summary_en: πFS is a novel distributed file system that leverages the mathematical properties of π (e.g., infinite, non-repeating digit sequences) to generate globally unique block identifiers and optimize replica placement. It aims to solve classical trade-offs in distributed storage — consistency, redundancy efficiency, and latency — by using π-based addressing for near-optimal load balancing and fault tolerance. Built in Rust with a POSIX-compatible interface, πFS targets cloud-native applications, big data pipelines, and decentralized infrastructure. The Hacker News discussion highlights skepticism about practical performance versus theoretical promises, as well as concerns over the computational overhead of π-based operations. If benchmarks validate its claims, πFS could challenge IPFS and Filecoin for use cases requiring high reliability without single points of failure, such as distributed ML training or blockchain node storage.

items.title: OpenCV 5 Is Here: The Biggest Leap in Years for Computer Vision
items.author: ternaus
items.summary_zh: OpenCV 5 是计算机视觉领域十年来的最大升级，正式支持 C++17 和 Python 3.12，核心模块全面重写以利用现代 CPU/GPU 指令集（如 AVX-512、NEON）。新增 transformer 和注意力机制后端，使得基于 ViT 的视觉模型推理速度提升 3-5 倍；DNN 模块集成 ONNX Runtime，可直接加载 PyTorch/TensorFlow 模型而无需手动转换。此外，OpenCV 5 引入了基于 CMake Presets 的新构建系统，简化交叉编译和移动端部署。对开发者而言，这意味着更低的集成成本、更高的推理性能以及更统一的多平台 API。社区反应积极，但也注意到部分旧接口被标记为弃用，迁移成本需评估。受益群体涵盖嵌入式视觉工程师（如 RISC-V 平台）、自动驾驶算法团队（需要实时性）以及学术研究者（可快速实验 SOTA 模型）。
items.summary_en: OpenCV 5 represents the most significant leap in the library's history, modernizing the codebase with mandatory C++17, full Python 3.12 support, and SIMD-optimized core routines (AVX-512, NEON). The DNN module now ships an ONNX Runtime backend, enabling direct inference of PyTorch/TensorFlow models without conversion, and a dedicated transformer attention engine that speeds up ViT-based pipelines by 3–5×. A revamped CMake-based build system with presets simplifies cross-compilation for embedded targets like RISC-V. For developers, this means lower integration friction, significantly faster inference on modern hardware, and a unified API across platforms. The Hacker News community largely welcomes the upgrade, though some express concern about deprecated legacy APIs. Key beneficiaries include engineers working on real-time ADAS, edge AI on mobile/embedded devices, and researchers needing quick prototyping of latest vision architectures.
